claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 19 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 8 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children that I'd like to ask about - Alex who is 10, Blake who is 19, and Charlie who is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. You have three children: Alex (10), Blake (19), and Charlie (2).

Let me ask you some questions to assess eligibility for each child. I'll start with a general question:

**Do all three children currently live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, yes. Alex and Blake live with me at home, but Charlie doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. So Alex and Blake live with you, but Charlie doesn't live with you.

Let me ask about Charlie first:

**Does Charlie live with someone else, and do you contribute financially towards Charlie's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Charlie lives with someone else, but I'm not currently contributing financially to their upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now let me ask about Blake, who is 19:

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training?** (For example, A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or similar approved courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Let me ask a few more questions about your situation.

**Is there anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me make sure I have everything I need:

- Alex (10) lives with you in the UK
- Blake (19) lives with you in the UK but is not in approved education or training
- Charlie (2) lives with someone else and you don't contribute to their upkeep
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them

Just one final question to confirm:

**Are you legally responsible for Alex and Blake (i.e., you're their parent or legal guardian)?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all the information I need. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your three children.

Let me make my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

Based on the information you've provided, here's the outcome for each of your children:

### ✅ **Alex (10 years old) - ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they are under 16, live with you in the UK, and you are their parent.

### ❌ **Blake (19 years old) - NOT ELIGIBLE**
You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake because, although they are under 20, they are not in approved education or training, which is a requirement for children aged 16-19.

### ❌ **Charlie (2 years old) - NOT ELIGIBLE**
You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie because they live with someone else and you are not contributing financially to their upkeep. To be eligible when a child lives elsewhere, you would need to contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards their care.

---

**What you can do:**
- You can claim Child Benefit for Alex
- If Blake returns to approved education or training in the future, you could become eligible for them again
- If you start contributing to Charlie's upkeep (at least the Child Benefit amount in food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation costs), you may be able to claim for Charlie as well

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 10 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are their parent and responsible for them. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Alex meets all these criteria, so you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 19 years old and lives with you. However, according to the rules, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in approved education or training, you do not qualify for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 2 years old but does not live with you. According to the rules, you could continue to get Child Benefit if your child lives with someone else, but only if you contribute to your child's upkeep by the same amount or more than the Child Benefit payment (contributions can include clothes, presents, food, pocket money, and financial contributions for accommodation). Since you are not currently contributing financially to Charlie's upkeep, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children. They are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex (10) who lives with them and is under 16. They are not eligible for Blake (19) because Blake is not in approved education or training. They are not eligible for Charlie (2) because Charlie lives with someone else and the claimant does not contribute to Charlie's upkeep.

Alex meets all these criteria, so you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 19 years old and lives with you. However, according to the rules, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in approved education or training, you do not qualify for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 2 years old but does not live with you. Since you are not currently contributing financially to Charlie's upkeep, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children. They are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex (10) who lives with them and is under 16. They are not eligible for Blake (19) because Blake is not in approved education or training. They are not eligible for Charlie (2) because Charlie lives with someone else and the claimant does not contribute to Charlie's upkeep.
